title: Inventory seed-system, adoption-survey, climate-variety release, and on-farm trial data sources for SSA and South Asia
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates seed-system indicators (TASAI), CGIAR variety-release catalogs, national agricultural survey adoption data, and on-farm trial results into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit crop-country coverage assessment.
inputs: National agricultural survey reports (LSMS, DHS, IHS)
Published on-farm trial studies for climate-resilient varieties
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, crop-country coverage, and documented adoption-measurement methodology.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering crop-country coverage, adoption-measurement methodology, and seed-system data availability.

title: Map climate-resilient seed availability and adoption gaps for at least two countries and two staple crops
outcome: A sub-national seed-availability and adoption-gap analysis for at least two countries and two crops, documenting variety-release status, seed-system infrastructure, farmer adoption rates, and on-farm yield-gap data where available.
inputs: TASAI or equivalent seed-system data for target countries
CGIAR variety-release catalogs for target crops
Published on-farm trial results for target crop-country combinations
expected_artifact: validation.md update with adoption-gap methodology and crop-country results.
validation_method: Replicator confirms seed-system data extraction, adoption-rate calculation, and yield-gap documentation for each crop-country combination.
done_condition: Adoption-gap analysis includes methodology, crop-country results, seed-system barrier documentation, yield-gap assessment, and replication status for at least two countries and two crops.

title: Classify seed-system barriers by tractability for target crop-country combinations
outcome: A barrier-classification analysis identifying which seed-system bottlenecks (multiplication, distribution, awareness, affordability, trust) are most tractable for each target crop-country combination, with explicit documentation of farmer-preference interactions with climate-resilience traits.
inputs: farmer-preference studies for target crops and regions
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with barrier-classification methodology and tractability assessment.
validation_method: Replicator confirms barrier-classification methodology, tractability assessment, and farmer-preference documentation for each crop-country combination.
done_condition: Barrier classification includes methodology, tractability ranking, farmer-preference interaction documentation, and replication status.

title: Red-team adoption-gap overstatement and yield-promise risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how adoption-gap maps could overstate the problem by ignoring farmer rational preferences, how yield-gap claims could promise performance that on-farm conditions cannot deliver, and how results could be used to justify seed subsidies that crowd out private-sector distribution.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every adoption-gap claim has seed-system data documentation, every yield-gap claim has on-farm-vs-on-station distinction, and every barrier classification has farmer-preference context. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every adoption-gap claim is either blocked, softened to data-limited language with explicit yield-gap caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match seed-system and agricultural extension decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the adoption-gap analysis would change any real decision for national agricultural research systems, seed companies, or extension services without creating perverse incentives.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for seed-system and extension audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that adoption-gap maps justify seed subsidies that crowd out private distribution or that yield-gap claims promise performance farmer conditions cannot deliver. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., CIMMYT seed-system program manager), named decision (e.g., seed-multiplication investment targeting), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for blanket seed-subsidy justification), and replicated field-reality review.
